contract.name: v2/autocomplete/program_activity.md
contract.description: Autocomplete search for program activity codes and names by partial code or text.

search_text.description: Case-insensitive search string matched against program activity codes and names.
search_text.constraints: Required non-empty string. Missing, null, or empty string values return HTTP 400. Non-string inputs pass validation but downstream processing raises a 500 HTML error.

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return. Defaults to 10 when omitted; large values can yield very large payloads.
limit.constraints: Optional. Accepts integers greater than or equal to zero, integer-like JSON numbers (decimals truncated toward zero), booleans (true treated as 1, false treated as 0), and numeric strings. Value 0 returns HTTP 200 with an empty list. Decimal strings and other non-numeric strings return 400. Negative or null values trigger a 500 HTML error.

results.description: Autocomplete matches ordered by backend relevance. Each entry includes both code and name strings; duplicate codes may appear with different names.

results.program_activity_name: MEAT AND POULTRY PROCESSING EXPANSION PROGRAM
MEAT AND POULTRY PROCESSING EXPANSION PROGRAM TA

body.detail: Missing one or more required request parameters: search_text

quirks: limit=0 returns HTTP 200 with an empty results array instead of rejecting the value.
Boolean limit values are coerced to integers (true becomes 1, false becomes 0).
Large limits (observed up to 20,000) return all matching rows without a documented hard cap, producing thousands of entries.
Whitespace-only search strings are accepted and matched literally against stored names.
Negative limit values or non-string search_text inputs lead to HTML 500 responses rather than structured validation errors.
Decimal limit values sent as numbers truncate toward zero; the same values sent as strings are rejected with HTTP 400.
Additional body fields beyond search_text and limit are silently ignored.
Duplicate program_activity_code entries may appear with different names and should be deduplicated client-side if needed.
risks: Invalid input types (e.g., non-string search_text or negative/null limit) can trigger 500 HTML errors that lack JSON structure; callers must validate before sending.
Large limit values can return thousands of rows without pagination; consumers should enforce their own caps or stream results.
Duplicate program_activity_code entries may require client-side deduplication depending on downstream expectations.
gaps: Result ordering appears relevance-based but no deterministic sort guarantees are documented or inferred from probes.
Maximum accepted limit remains unverified beyond 20,000; higher values were not tested.

probes.notes: Positive limit reduces result count accordingly.
Zero limit yields empty results without error.
Negative limit produces an HTML 500 error.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Missing one or more required request parameters: search_text"}

probes.notes: Decimal JSON number truncated toward zero.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Limit request parameter is not a valid, positive integer"}

probes.notes: Decimal limit sent as string rejected with 400.
Large limit returned 7,427 matches without a hard cap error.

mismatches: Docs allow generic numeric limit values, but the API only accepts integer-like inputs; decimal or negative strings error despite being described as numbers.
gaps: Result ordering appears relevance-based but no deterministic sort guarantees are documented or inferred from probes.
Maximum accepted limit remains unverified beyond 20,000; higher values were not tested.
risks: Invalid input types (e.g., non-string search_text or negative/null limit) can trigger 500 HTML errors that lack JSON structure; callers must validate before sending.
Large limit values can return thousands of rows without pagination; consumers should enforce their own caps or stream results.
Duplicate program_activity_code entries may require client-side deduplication depending on downstream expectations.
